_meta.author: refine-scorecard skill (research session)
_meta.note: Evidence-based finalization of Kim Driscoll (MA Lt. Gov, D). Replaces party-default archetype FALSEs/seeds with cited evidence; nulls unsupported cells. Confirmed office + 2026 reelection (Healey-Driscoll launched 2026-01-20). State tier.

profile.background: Lieutenant Governor of Massachusetts since Jan 2023 (Healey-Driscoll administration, first all-women executive team in U.S. history). Former Mayor of Salem, MA (2006-2023). Launched 2026 reelection campaign with Gov. Healey on 2026-01-20.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Evidence-based refinement. Confirmed incumbent Lt. Gov. + 2026 reelection (westernmassnews.com 2026-01-20; maurahealey.com launch). Replaced party-default archetype seed with cited HARD/SOFT evidence; nulled unsupported cells per evidence-only protocol.

kim-driscoll.notes_set: EVIDENCE DOSSIER (2026-06-17). OFFICE/CANDIDACY: Sitting Lt. Gov. of MA (Healey-Driscoll, since 1/2023); launched 2026 reelection 1/20/2026 (HARD). LIFE: Strong abortion-rights record — 'Abortion care is healthcare and this fight is not over'; administration adopted emergency reproductive-health regs, awarded $1.8M for abortion access, signed updated shield law; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocacy Fund of MA for reelection (HARD). No evidence on personhood/IVF; Gov. Healey backs medical aid in dying but NO Driscoll-specific quote found (null on q3). MARRIAGE: As Salem mayor passed transgender non-discrimination ordinance, 100% HRC Municipal Equality Index; administration reaffirmed gender-affirming-care protections incl. for minors; 100% LGBTQ advocacy record (HARD FALSE on all marriage cells). FAMILY/EDU: administration condemned dismantling of US Dept of Ed; gender-affirming care for minors protected (FALSE q1); no school-choice/homeschool evidence (null). RELIGIOUS LIBERTY (HIGH-VALUE HARD): As Salem mayor 2014 terminated Gordon College (Christian college) contract for Old Town Hall after the college sought a religious exemption from an LGBT non-discrimination order — explicitly declined a Christian conscience exemption ('religious freedom does not afford you the right to impose those beliefs') = FALSE on christian_liberty q1. ECONOMY: co-files balanced annual budgets (routine governance, not a balanced-budget amendment — null q2); administration drives green-bank/ESG-style climate finance (SOFT FALSE q4); NO CBDC evidence (null q0). ELECTIONS: NO Driscoll-specific evidence (all null). PUBLIC JUSTICE: administration funds police body cameras, police-academy scholarships, public-safety staffing — affirmative but framed around 'transparency/accountability/equity', not a 'back the blue/oppose defunding' stance; held below the affirmative-pro-police TRUE bar (null q0). IMMIGRATION: administration sued to block ICE funding actions, filed bill to keep ICE out of schools/hospitals/churches, launched ICE-misconduct reporting portal, pro-migrant-shelter spending (HARD FALSE on enforcement/sanctuary cells). FEDERALISM: embraces federal funding (sues to keep it), no 10th-Amendment/anti-commandeering record in rubric direction (null). GUNS: publicly backed MA's 2024 firearms law (assault-weapons/LCM ban, expanded red-flag) — Driscoll: 'Gun technology and trends are constantly evolving, and it's important that our laws evolve along with them' (HARD FALSE q1).

1.note: HARD: pro-abortion-rights. 'Abortion care is healthcare and this fight is not over'; administration adopted emergency reproductive-health regs and awarded $1.8M for abortion access; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocacy Fund of MA for reelection. Opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on embryonic stem cell / IVF embryo discard.

3.note: Gov. Healey backs MA medical aid in dying, but NO Driscoll-specific position found. Null.

4.note: HARD: accepted Planned Parenthood Advocacy Fund of MA endorsement (abortion-industry advocacy backing) for reelection.

0.note: No explicit statement on marriage definition; her LGBTQ record implies support for same-sex marriage but no direct quote. Null on this specific cell.

1.note: HARD: career-long LGBTQ ally; 100% HRC Municipal Equality Index as Salem mayor; supports same-sex marriage/civil rights. Opposes the rubric position.

2.note: HARD: as Salem mayor passed transgender non-discrimination ordinance; administration reaffirmed gender-affirming-care protections. Affirms transgender ideology, contrary to rubric.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: HARD: actively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy (gender-affirming-care protections incl. minors; honored repeatedly by LGBTQ orgs as ally).

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on school choice/vouchers/homeschooling.

1.note: HARD: administration protects gender-affirming care for minors and providers, opposite of parental-consent/notification rubric position.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a Driscoll position on CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curricula specifically.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on porn age-verification.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on faith-based adoption agency placement policy (her record is hostile to LGBT-exemptions broadly but no specific adoption-placement action).

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on public profession of Christ.

1.note: HARD (high-value): As Salem mayor (2014) terminated Gordon College's (Christian college) Old Town Hall contract after the college sought a religious exemption from an LGBT non-discrimination order. Driscoll: 'religious freedom does not afford you the right to impose those beliefs upon others.' Documented refusal of a Christian conscience exemption.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on compelled-speech / pronoun mandates.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on public-square Christian symbols / prayer / Sabbath protections.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on CBDC stance.

2.note: Co-files balanced annual state budgets (routine governance), but NO position on a balanced-budget constitutional amendment. Null.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on usury limits / tithe-friendly tax structures.

4.note: SOFT: administration drives green-bank / ESG-aligned climate finance (nation's first green bank, decarbonization grants). Embraces the ESG/sustainable-investment agenda the rubric opposes. (Administration-level, no Driscoll quote specifically on ESG.)

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a Driscoll position on paper ballots / voting machines.

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on photo voter ID.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on single-day in-person voting.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a Driscoll position on mail-in voting / drop boxes / harvesting.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on private election funding.

0.note: Administration funds police body cameras, a police-academy scholarship program, and public-safety staffing — affirmative engagement, but framed around 'transparency/accountability/equity', not a 'back the blue / oppose defunding' stance. Held below the affirmative-pro-police TRUE bar. Null (not credited, not penalized).

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a Driscoll position on cashless bail / rogue prosecutors.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on victim restitution / proportional punishment.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of 10th-Amendment / anti-commandeering action.

1.note: Administration sues to KEEP federal funding flowing (embraces federal money), opposite of declining federal strings. No rubric-direction evidence. Null.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of resisting federal overreach on guns/life/education/religious liberty in the conservative direction (administration aligns with federal progressive policy). Null.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND in rubric direction.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a Driscoll position on a physical border barrier (federal matter).

1.note: HARD: administration filed legislation to keep ICE out of schools/hospitals/courthouses/churches and launched an ICE-misconduct reporting portal; opposes mass deportation enforcement.

2.note: HARD: administration actively obstructs federal immigration enforcement (anti-ICE legislation + portal; pro-migrant-shelter spending), the de facto sanctuary posture the rubric opposes.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on mandatory E-Verify.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on birthright citizenship / foreign farmland ownership.

1.note: HARD: publicly backed MA's 2024 firearms law (assault-weapons & large-capacity-magazine ban, expanded red-flag/ERPO). Driscoll: 'Gun technology and trends are constantly evolving, and it's important that our laws evolve along with them.' Directly supports the bans/red-flag laws the rubric opposes.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on repeal of NFA/GCA (federal matter).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on castle doctrine / stand-your-ground.
